conversation.content:
Reconcile the following columns. Document has 14 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

COLUMNS:

---
Column 1: OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment
Definition: What percentage of total patients in the experimental arm survived at the latest follow-up? If reported, include the rate (percentage) and any accompanying details. If multiple arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="Abiraterone trial: 48% (43-52) at 84 months; Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: Not reported" | page=9 | modality=text | reasoning="The text on page 9 explicitly states the 84-month overall survival rate for the abiraterone treatment arm. For the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, the text provides median overall survival but not a specific percentage at the latest follow-up (72 months) or 84 months."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="Agent did not submit"

  A: value="Abiraterone trial: 30% (95% CI 26-34) at 84 months; Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: Not reported" | page=9 | modality=text | reasoning="The text on page 9 explicitly states the 84-month overall survival rate for the abiraterone control arm. For the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, the text provides median overall survival but not a specific percentage at the latest follow-up (72 months) or 84 months."

Figure 4: Secondary efficacy endpoint analysis
Shaded regions represent 95% Cls. Survival curves for the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial are capped at 84 months due to shorter follow-up than in the abiraterone trial. Abiraterone refers to abiraterone acetate and prednisolone. Prostate cancer-specific survival (A, B), failure free survival (C, D) and metastatic progression free survival (E, F) in the abiraterone and abiraterone and enzalutamide trials.



(difference 10.2 months [6.8-13.5]; p<0.0001) and
51.3 months (48.8-53.8) with standard of care versus
58.6 months (56.0-61.1) with addition of abiraterone
and enzalutamide (difference 7.3 months [3.7-10.9];
p<0.0001). At 84 months in the abiraterone trial,



overall survival was 30% (95% CI 26–34) with standard
of care versus 48% (43–52) with standard of care
plus abiraterone.
In prespecified subgroup analyses by stratification
factors, there was an interaction with NSAID or aspirin

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.value: Abiraterone trial: 30% (95% CI 26-34) at 84 months; Abiraterone and enzalutamide trial: Not reported
OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.reasoning: Page 9 explicitly states 'At 84 months in the abiraterone trial, overall survival was 30% (95% CI 26–34) with standard of care versus...'. For the abiraterone and enzalutamide trial, median overall survival is given, but not a percentage at a specific time point, so 'Not reported' is correct for that part. Therefore, A is correct and B is wrong.
